original_abstract:
This comprehensive study explores the multidimensional landscape of data governance, tracing its evolution from foundational definitions to its critical role in the modern digital economy. We begin by establishing a robust conceptual framework, examining essential principles, industry-standard models, and best practices that underpin effective governance. The research delves into the technical and operational pillars of the field, specifically focusing on data quality management, security protocols, and the development of rigorous institutional policies.

Beyond technical implementation, this paper emphasizes the human and organizational elements necessary for success, including the definition of strategic roles, the cultivation of a data-centric culture through proactive leadership, and the adoption of emerging stewardship models. Through a series of cross-industry case studies, we analyze how governance strategies are tailored to diverse regulatory and legal environments. Furthermore, we address the practicalities of tool selection, maturity assessment, and the use of metrics to quantify impact. The study concludes by anticipating future trends and addressing the socio-technical challenges organizations must navigate to maintain data integrity and security in an increasingly complex global landscape.
humanized_abstract:
Data governance—the conceptual history and multifaceted nature The article explores the historical and multifaceted development of the concept of data governance from its initial definitions to the present-day economy. Initially, we introduce the theoretical fundamentals and governing principles of data governance, while describing generally accepted models and practices. Special attention is given to technological and operational aspects of data governance including data quality, data protection, and implementing internal regulations.

This paper focuses on the people and process aspects of information management, such as defining information roles, leadership to foster an information-based culture, and new models of stewardship, in addition to the technology itself. Through a series of cross-industry case studies of governance practices for specific business or operational environments, this study delves into the specifics of tool choices, maturity levels and metrics for measurement of the effects of information management practices. Finally, it speculates on the future, while at the same time examining the socio-technical issues that will have to be addressed in order to continue to maintain data quality and security in a rapidly evolving global environment.
